text.p: parts of Lower South East district.
The State Emergency Service advises that people should:
* Move vehicles under cover or away from trees;
* Secure or put away loose items around your property.
* Stay indoors, away from windows, while conditions are severe.

warning-info.text: The next Severe Weather Warning will be issued by 11:00 am ACST Thursday.

text.p: Check https://www.bom.gov.au/weather-and-climate/warnings-and-alerts. Warnings are also available through TV and Radio broadcasts or call 1300 659 210. The Bureau and State Emergency Service would appreciate warnings being broadcast regularly.

forecast-period.text: Damaging winds developing over coastal parts of the Lower South East Thursday evening.

text.p: Weather Situation: A cold front is moving east out of the state early this morning, before a deep low pressure system expected to pass to the south later on Thursday.

The immediate threat of DAMAGING WINDS has eased, but DAMAGING WEST TO SOUTHWESTERLY WINDS averaging 50-65 km/h with peak gusts of around 100 km/h are likely over the Lower South East coast during Thursday evening, easing later on Friday morning.
Locations which may be affected include Robe and Millicent.
Severe weather is no longer occurring in the Mount Lofty Ranges, Lower Eyre Peninsula, Yorke Peninsula, Kangaroo Island and Murraylands districts and the warning for these districts is CANCELLED.

The immediate threat of DAMAGING WINDS has eased, but DAMAGING WEST TO SOUTHWESTERLY WINDS averaging 50-65 km/h with peak gusts of around 100 km/h are likely over the Lower South East coast during Thursday evening, easing later on Friday morning.
Locations which may be affected include Robe and Millicent.
Severe weather is no longer occurring in the Mount Lofty Ranges, Lower Eyre Peninsula, Yorke Peninsula, Kangaroo Island and Murraylands districts and the warning for these districts is CANCELLED.
